2.  [Setting Up the Portal](setting-up-the-api-portal)

    Use the guided setup to set up the portal and apply styling options to brand your portal. After
    the setup and styling are complete, the portal is created.

## Before You Begin

Before creating your portal, we recommend that you have the following information:

- Review the [Prerequisites](getting-started-with-api-experience-hub)
- Image for company logo
- Color scheme for styling your portal
- Image to use as a header for your portal
---

# Customizing Your Portal Using the Experience Builder

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After the portal is created using the out-of-the-box styling templates, you can further customize it
using the
[Experience Builder](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.community_designer_overview.htm&type=5).
The Builder customizes a Salesforce Experience Cloud site with your desired brand identity, look and
feel, and site structure by providing control over the siteâs components, themes, and pages, and its
settings for general properties, themes, and SEO.

From the **Catalog management** page, the **Preview and publish your portal** link opens your site
for editing each page in your portal, creating new pages, and adding and editing the components on
each page. Administrators enter the Salesforce Experience Builder with a shared identity between all
administrators. Since the identity of the administrator is shared therefore, API Experience Hub
canât determine the individual administratorâs action in the shared account. If the administrator is
already logged in to the Salesforce organization, their previous session isnât retained when they
click the **Preview and publish your portal** button.

The Builderâs interface contains the pages list, component palette, theme menu, page structure menu,
and settings menu.

Click **Preview** to view your changes and to see the portal experience for your developer
community.

Click **Home** to view the Carousel with all of the added services. Click **Services** to view the
Catalog, search and filter services, and select a service to get more details from the service
details page. The service details page displays metadata in the upper section and the API overview
and specification in the lower section. Publish any updates by clicking **Publish**.

## See Also

- [Customizing the Portal Look-and-Feel](look-and-feel-customization)
- [API Experience Hub Lightning Components](api-experience-hub-lightning-components)
- [Using the Builder for Common Customizations](common-builder-customizations)
- [Advanced Styling with CSS](advanced-styling-with-css)
- [Previewing and Publishing Your Portal](previewing-and-publishing-your-portal)
---

# Deactivating Your Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can make your published portal inaccessible to users by deactivating the portal site. The portal
is still considered active but it canât be accessed publicly. You can continue to make changes using
the Builder and republish the site later for users to access.

To deactivate your portal site, follow these steps:

1.  From a **Manage your portal** page, click **Preview and publish your portal**.
2.  Click the **Builder** icon.
3.  From **My Workspaces**, click **Administration**.
4.  Click **Settings**.
5.  Click **Deactivate**.

    Your site is inactive and offline.

To active your site, follow steps 1 to 4, then click **Active**.

## See Also

- [Disconnecting the Portal](disconnecting-the-portal)
---

# Disconnecting the Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Platform can connect to only one Salesforce organization, and you can create only one
portal for that organization. To create a new portal, you must disconnect the current portal from
your Salesforce organization. This process unlinks Anypoint Platform from your Salesforce
organization. You can then link your Anypoint Platform account to a new Salesforce organization.

If you reconnect the portal (for example, after creating a new connection), you must reconfigure
SSO, republish all services to the portal, and reconfigure group mapping. There is no process to
restore the previous configuration automatically. Note that styling metadata is retained when
reconnecting to the same Salesforce organization.

There is no supported way to change the user who created the connection in API Experience Hub. To
assign the connection to a different user, you must disconnect and create a new connection. For more
information, see [Assigning Permissions](assigning-permissions).

## Before You Disconnect

Disconnecting your portal removes artifacts from the API Experience Hub database and unlinks
Anypoint Platform from your Salesforce organization.

These artifacts are permanently removed from API Experience Hub:

- All published assets and their visibility settings
- All custom user groups
- All group mapping configurations (for both custom and default user groups)
- The Single Sign-On (SSO) configuration

Before you disconnect, record the following information so you can restore your portal configuration
after reconnecting:

1.  Record your published services and visibility settings.
    1.  From the **Catalog management** page, select the **Services in the portal** tab.
    2.  Record a list of all services in the portal.

        You must republish these services under the new connection.

    3.  For each service, record its visibility settings (including instance and user visibility per
        version) so you can restore them after reconnecting. For more information, see
        [Make Versions Visible](managing-apis#make-versions-visible).

2.  Record your user groups information.
    1.  From the **User groups** page, record all user groups and their details (for example, group
        names and members).

        For more information, see [Manage User Groups](managing-users#manage-user-groups).

    2.  For each group, record the corresponding identity provider name.

        You need this information when you re-enable SSO and reconfigure group mapping. For more
        information, see
        [managing-users.adoc#map-user-groups-to-external-identity-providers](managing-users#map-user-groups-to-external-identity-providers)
        and [Configuring SSO](configuring-sso).

## Disconnect the Portal

1.  From the **Manage your portal** page, click **Settings**.
2.  Click **Disconnect Salesforce Org**.
3.  To confirm, click **Disconnect Salesforce Org** in the message.

## See Also

- [Connecting to Salesforce](connecting-to-salesforce)
- [Assigning Permissions](assigning-permissions)
- [Catalog Management](managing-apis)
- [Managing Users](managing-users)
---

# Discovering and Consuming Services

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Using a self-service portal, developers and partners can quickly access applications, interact with
documentation, and build products from existing data. API Experience Hub portals enable developers
to find, consume, and reuse services that are essential to building their products.

For more information, see these topics:

- [Registering for Access to a Portal](registering-for-access-to-portal)
- [Discovering Services](discovering-apis)
- [Searching for Services](searching-for-apis)
- [Viewing Service Details](viewing-api-details)
- [Requesting Access to a Service Instance](requesting-access-to-an-api-instance)
- [My Applications](my-applications)
- [Downloading an API Specification](downloading-an-api-specification)
- [Using the API Console](using-the-api-console)
---

# Discovering Services

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Home page or landing page of a portal contains the carousel. The Carousel offers consumers an
interactive way to explore all the available services. The carousel shows service cards in rows and
columns, and divided into pages, and shows controls to filter the displayed services by type and
search terms. Each card shows a service. With the carousel, you can view and explore large numbers
of versions.

The Services page contains the catalog of all the services in the portal. The searchable catalog
shows a horizontally scrollable set of service cards. Each card shows a service. The service icon,
name, and description are shown based on the service information from Exchange.

## See Also

- [Searching for Services](searching-for-apis)
- [Viewing Service Details](viewing-api-details)
---

# Downloading an API Specification

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

A copy of an API specification can be downloaded from the API Details page by clicking **Download**.

The download file type varies depending on the API:

- AsyncAPI

  Downloads as a .json file.

- GraphQL

  Downloads as .json schema file.

- gRPC

  Downloads as a .proto type file.

- REST API RAML

  If you choose As RAML, the original RAML file downloads as a zip file. If you choose As OAS, the
  API specification is downloaded as a zip file containing a JSON file for the specification in OAS
  2.0 format.

- REST API OAS

  If you choose As RAML, the API specification is downloaded as a zip file containing a RAML file
  for the specification. If you choose As OAS, a zip file containing a file in the original format
  of either JSON or YAML is downloaded.

- SOAP API WSDL

  Downloads as a WSDL file.

## See Also

- [Using the API Console](using-the-api-console)
- [Simulating API Calls Using the Mocking Service](simulating-api-calls-using-the-mocking-service)
- [Testing APIs Using an API Instance](testing-apis-using-an-api-instance)
---

# Edit Service Display Details

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following information that displays for a service can be edited:

- Description
- Instance visibility
- Version visibility
- Icon image

  Image files must be .png,

To edit the information that displays for a service:

1.  From the catalog, click a service card.

    The Catalog management page displays the information of the service.

2.  From the **Actions** options, select **Edit display details**.
3.  To change the description, enter a new asset description in the **Description** field.
4.  To change the instance visibility, select an option from the drop-down list.
5.  To change the version visibility, select an option from the drop-down list for the corresponding
    version
6.  To change the icon, click **Upload file** and select an approved image file type.
7.  To cancel the changes, click **Cancel**.
8.  To save the changes, click **Save**.
---

# Enabling Portal Access to Salesforce Users

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Give existing Salesforce users access to a portal so they can view your portal. Admins can enable
access for Salesforce users without recreating profiles, permission sets, or setting up SSO.

Use the Builder to configure access. After a user requests access, you can approve the request from
the **Pending registration requests** page from **Manage users**. An email is sent to the user with
instructions about how to log in.

## Enable Portal Access to Salesforce Users

Enable Salesforce users to access the portal.

1.  Enable Salesforce users to log in:
    1.  From the Builder, click the **Home** icon > **Administration** > **Login & Registration**.
    2.  From the **Login Page Setup** section, select **Allow employees to log in directly to an
        Experience Cloud Site** and click **Save**.

2.  Add the API Experience Hub Member User permission set:
    1.  From **Administration**, click **Members**.
    2.  From the **Select Permission Sets** section, select **API Experience Hub Member User from
        Available Permission Sets** in **Available Permission Sets** and click **Add** to populate
        the permission set in **Selected Permission Sets**.
    3.  Click **Save**.

3.  Add the **Existing User Request Access** Lightning component to the portal page:
    1.  From the Builder, click the Lightning component icon, select **Existing User Request
        Access** from **Custom Components**, and drag the component to where you want it located in
        a portal page.
    2.  Click the component on the page and enter these fields:
        - **Label**: Enter **Username**.
        - **Placeholder**: Enter **Enter your username**.
        - **Redirect to**: Enter the page name to redirect to after a user requests access. The
          default is to redirect users to the **Registration request successful** page.
        - **Button Label**: Enter a name for the access button.

4.  Approve or reject the user:
    1.  Go to **User management** in **Catalog management** and select **Pending registration
        requests**.
    2.  Locate the user in the list and click **Approve**.

## Remove Portal Access From Salesforce Users

Remove access to the portal access for existing Salesforce users who should no longer access your
portal.

1.  In Salesforce, go to **Setup** > **Users** > **Users** and select a specific user.
2.  From **Permission Set Assignments**, delete the **API Experience Hub Member User** permission
    set and save your changes.

    The user wonât be able to log in to the portal. In the portal, the userâs name wonât appear in
    the **User management** > **Portal Members** list.

## See Also

- [Existing User Request Access Lightning component](api-experience-hub-lightning-components#existing-user-request-access)
- [Managing Users](managing-users)
---

# Enabling Single Sign-On for Your Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Administrators or security teams can implement an external identity provider (IdP) as a login option
for an API Experience Hub portal. Single sign-on (SSO) authentication provides a seamless login
experience for your customers with your organizationâs unique branding. Your users can log in to
your portal without having to remember another username and password or entering login credentials
multiple times.

After a member of your portal visits the portal page and selects the SSO option, theyâre redirected
to the identity provider login page to authenticate. The SSO authentication process uses the
identity provider to check the identity of a user. If the login is successful, the user that aligns
with the user profile is created in Access Management. The API Experience Hub portal home page
appears for the user to browse and discover services.

API Experience Hub supports multiple IdPs for SSO.

Access Management configures identity management for SSO for Anypoint Platform. For more information
about identity providers, see
[Identity Management](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/external-identity) in Access
Management.

## Configuring SSO

The high-level steps for setting up SSO for your portal vary depending on your use case.

- [Step 1: Enable SSO for Your Portal](configuring-sso#step-1-enable-sso-for-your-portal)

  In this step, you create and configure an application in the identity provider.

  You can configure an identity provider for Anypoint Platform and Salesforce can be configured
  using OpenID Connect or SAML (Single Sign-on Settings). Microsoft Azure OpenID Connect isnât
  supported.

- [Step 2: Add Salesforce Identity Providers](configuring-sso#step-2-add-salesforce-identity-providers)

  In this step, you add and enable the Salesforce identity provider from API Experience Hub UI. Once
  the identity provider is enabled, users can see the login option from the portalâs login page.

- [Step 3: Add Group Mappings](configuring-sso#add-group-mapping)

  In this step, you map the groups created in the Salesforce identity provider that you added to the
  corresponding team profile for the portal.

- [Step 4: Test the SSO Configuration](configuring-sso#test-sso)

  In this step, you test the SSO configuration that you just set up for the portal.

This diagram shows the overall steps to take in the external identity provider, Anypoint Platform,
and Salesforce to set up your single sign-on configuration:

![A diagram showing steps and applications required to configure SSO](_images/aeh-sso-flow2.png)

## Out-of-the-Box Configurations

API Experience Hub provides the following out-of-the-box configurations to assist the process of
enabling SSO:

- anypoint_idp_id

  The custom claim or SAML attribute provides the information thatâs received from the identity
  provider that determines which Anypoint Platform identity provider to map a user to.

- AEH Users - ${salesforceOrganizationId}

  The default Anypoint Experience Hub identity provider thatâs created in Anypoint Platform to map
  all self-registered users. This identity provider is used when the aeh_portal_idp isnât set in the
  identity provider. Self-registered users are mapped with the default identity provider.

- AEH Portal - ${salesforceOrganizationId}\_$\*{salesforceCommunityId}

  This solution creates a team called AEH Portal in Access Management automatically. This team
  includes administrators, guests, and members profiles. You select this team when adding the group
  mapping.

- AEHPortalRegistrationHandler.cls

  A single Apex class is provided in the API Experience Hub package called
  AEHPortalRegistrationHandler.cls that is compatible with SAML and OpenID Connect.

  The registration handler is Apex code that runs when a user logs in to the Salesforce Experience
  Cloud site. You select this handler when configuring an authentication provider for Salesforce.
  When the identity provider is configured properly, Salesforce passes the portal ID to the
  AEHPortalRegistrationHandler. If the user does not exist, the registration handler creates the
  userâs identity in Salesforce. If the user exists, the handler updates the user metadata based on
  information sent by the identity provider, for example, the userâs first name and last name. The
  registration handler uses the portal ID in Salesforce to load specific configurations by the
  portal. For example, the registration handler creates the user in the profile and account related
  to the portal from the NetworkSelfRegistration object.

## Basic Terminology

The following terms are referenced in this topic:

Authentication Provider

An authentication provider is a framework that allows you to connect Salesforce to a third party for
authorized data access, authentication, or both, depending on the protocol. Authentication providers
can implement OAuth 2.0 to authorize Salesforce to access third-party data. Or they can implement
OpenID Connect or custom authentication protocols to support both third-party data access and
authentication.

When youâre using authentication providers, Salesforce is always the relying party. If the
authentication provider implements OpenID Connect, we refer to the third party as the OpenID
provider. If it implements a custom authentication protocol, we call the third party the identity
provider.

Identity Provider

An identity provider acts as a trusted service that authenticates a userâs identity.

OpenID Connect

OpenID Connect is an open standard authentication protocol built on OAuth 2.0. With OpenID Connect,
the relying party and OpenID provider can exchange information about who a user is and what they can
do with a service.

OpenID Provider

In OpenID Connect, an identity provider is called an OpenID provider. It authenticates users as
requested by the relying party.

Relying Party

In OpenID Connect and custom authentication protocols, a service provider is called a relying party,
though some use the terms interchangeably. It relies on the OpenID provider or identity provider for
authentication.

Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML)

SAML is an open standard authentication protocol that you can use to implement SSO in your
Salesforce org. SAML allows identity providers and service providers to securely exchange user
information, enabling user authentication between services.

Single Sign-On

Single sign-on (SSO) is an authentication method that enables users to access multiple applications
with one login and one set of credentials. For example, after users log in to your org, they can
automatically access all apps from the App Launcher. You can set up your Salesforce or Anypoint
Platform organization to trust a third-party identity provider to authenticate users. Or you can
configure a third-party app to rely on your org for authentication.

## See Also

- [User Roles for Configuring SSO](user-roles-permissions-and-profiles#user-roles-for-configuring-sso)
- [Gathering Setup Information for SSO](gathering-set-up-information)
- [Configuring SSO](configuring-sso)
- [Troubleshooting Single Sign-on Errors](troubleshoot-sso-errors)
- [Managing Users](managing-users)
---

# Gathering Setup Information for SSO

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use these sections to gather the information youâll need to set up SSO.

You might need to collaborate with your security team or other team members if you donât have access
to this information. For more information, see
[User Roles for Configuring SSO](user-roles-permissions-and-profiles#user-roles-for-configuring-sso).

## Username Assignment Strategy for a Shared Identity

If an identity provider is configured for your Anypoint Platform organization, review the username
assignment strategy because it can be different for the portal and Anypoint Platform users.

To have a single identity between the portal and Anypoint Platform, the format for the username
assignment must be the same. If the username strategy doesnât match, reconcile the username format
by adding attributes for `anypoint_idp_id` and `anypoint_username`. Then, map this information to
the Anypoint Platform identity provider that you are using for SSO.

For more information about adding custom claims when the identity provider is already configured but
not in the required format, see _Required Claims and Attributes for SSO_.

## Required Claims and Attributes for SSO

Be aware of API Experience Hub requirements for claims or attributes when creating a new application
for the portal in the identity provider.

Configuring the required user claims in the identity provider ensures that the user information is
mapped correctly to Salesforce and Anypoint Platform users.

The **AEHPortalRegistrationHandler** requires specific user information from the identity provider
to create or update a user. The registration handler uses required claims to extract user
information. This table lists the required information and which claims the information is extracted
from. If more than one claim exists for the same value, the registration handler looks for the first
expected claim in the order it appears in the table:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Expected Claims</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>First Name</p></td><td><p>given_name, first_name, firstname, and firstName</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Last Name</p></td><td><p>family_name, last_name, lastname, and lastName</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Email</p></td><td><p>email, email_address, and emailAddress</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>preferred_username, federation_identifier, email, sub, and NameId</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Groups</p></td><td><p>groups</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

To configure SSO when the identity provider is already configured for Anypoint Platform, use this
information:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th><th>Expected Values</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Anypoint Idp Id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map identities to the specific identity provider in Anypoint Platform.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>anypoint_idp_id</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Anypoint Username</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Explicitly specify the Anypoint Platform user if the userâs identity exists in Anypoint Platform, but the username is different than the Salesforce user.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>anypoint_username</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

If the required information is not in the supported format, create a new custom attribute or claim
with the supported format. For information, see the documentation for the identity provider.

## See Also

- [User Roles for Configuring SSO](user-roles-permissions-and-profiles#user-roles-for-configuring-sso)
- [Configuring SSO](configuring-sso)
---

# Getting Started with API Experience Hub

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Ensure you have the required accounts, access, and setup, then start using API Experience Hub.

## Before You Begin

Verify that you have the following required accounts and access:

- Anypoint Platform organization in production
- Salesforce organization
- Organization Administrator of the main organization
- You must either have the System Administrator profile or be a Salesforce user with the
  AEH_Anypoint permission set assigned in the Salesforce organization that youâre connecting to
  during the linking process. For more information, see
  [Assigning Permissions](assigning-permissions).

  The process of creating your portal includes connecting with Salesforce Experience Cloud to link
  your Anypoint Platform organization to your Salesforce organization.

- API Experience Hub license
- Minimum of 2000 External Identity licenses

  The current package has a minimum of 100 services access requests, which require a minimum of 2000
  External Identity licenses.

To set up an organization, you can have the following Salesforce editions:

- Unlimited Edition
- Enterprise Edition
- Performance Edition of Salesforce

If you donât have the required accounts and access, issues might occur during the Salesforce linking
process when creating your portal.

## Start Using API Experience Hub

The following users can get started with these tasks:

**Administrators**:

Administrators can create a portal in these easy steps:

1.  [Assign permissions](assigning-permissions)

    Set up the necessary permissions for administrators to connect to Salesforce and create a
    portal.

2.  [Connect to Salesforce](connecting-to-salesforce)

    The instance can be a new or existing Salesforce organization. To connect, specify the
    environment type and enter the custom Salesforce domain if you have one, log in, allow the
    permissions, and confirm the account.

3.  [Set up and style your portal](setting-up-the-api-portal)

    Once youâre connected to Salesforce, set up and style the portalâs out-of-the-box pages with
    logos, fonts, color schemes, and more.

4.  [Manage your portal](managing-your-portal)

    Manage services by adding agents, APIs, LLMs, and MCP servers from Exchange and setting the
    visibility for the service versions. Manage users by approving access requests from guest users.

5.  [Preview and publish your portal](previewing-and-publishing-your-portal)

    Publish the portal to make it available to your community.

**Developers**:

Developers can discover and consume services using these tasks:

1.  [Registering for Access to a Portal](registering-for-access-to-portal)
2.  [Searching for Services](searching-for-apis)
3.  [Viewing Service Details](viewing-api-details)
4.  [Requesting Access to a Service Instance](requesting-access-to-an-api-instance)
5.  [View your applications that hold a contract with a service](my-applications)
6.  [Downloading an API Specification](downloading-an-api-specification)
7.  [Inspect API Console specification and documentation](using-the-api-console)

## See Also

- [User Roles, Permissions, and Profiles](user-roles-permissions-and-profiles)
- [Licensing](licensing)
- [Connecting to Salesforce](connecting-to-salesforce)
---

# API Experience Hub Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

API Experience Hub provides a unified portal for discovering and consuming APIs and AI agents. You
can use it as an internal user, a partner, or an external developer.

Service providers can share, promote, and engage with their developer community. Developers and
partners can find, explore, and consume services designed anywhere from a single place.

API Experience Hub is the next-generation evolution of API Community Manager for creating and
managing portals. The portal administrator can use out-of-the-box templates to build developer
portals in minutes and publish services like agents, APIs, LLMs, and MCP servers from Anypoint
Exchange to any audience. Anypoint Platform fully manages the portal deployment, administration, and
the catalog and user management options so you donât need any Salesforce knowledge to start.

API Experience Hub provides the following advantages for:

**Portal administrators**:

- Set up portals quickly and easily
- Onboard services and setting version visibility
- Manage users and access requests from guest users
- Preview and iterate on the portal
- Perform advanced customizations using Salesforce Experience Builder
- Enable Chatter Feed tracking to engage with your portal community
- Use Google Analytics to gain valuable insights into how consumers are using your services and
  portal

**Developers**:

- Self-service experience to discover services and build applications
- Request access to an instance to register a client application
- Interactive documentation to understand how to use and integrate services
- Customized and connected hub to interact with services
- Collaborate with members of the portal community

Watch the API Experience Hub Product Spotlight video to see a quick overview.

![](https://play.vidyard.com/T4bQEfxC74Qg3k92wRgt7b.jpg)

## Steps to Create a Portal

An administrator can create a custom portal using these steps:

1.  Connect to a Salesforce instance.

    The instance can be a new or existing Salesforce organization. To connect, specify the
    environment type and enter the custom Salesforce domain if you have one, log in, allow the
    permissions, and confirm the account.

2.  Set up your portal.

    Once you connect to Salesforce, choose how to set up the portal and style the portalâs out of
    the box pages with logos, fonts, color schemes, and more.

3.  Manage your portal.

    Manage the catalog by adding services from Exchange and setting the visibility for the versions.
    Manage users by approving access requests from guest users.

4.  Publish the portal.

    Publish the portal to make it available to your community.

## Portal Pages

Each API Experience Hub portal includes a Home, Services page, and an Service Details page. The
portal administrator can customize each page to fit the organizationâs needs. For more information,
see [Setting Up the Portal](setting-up-the-api-portal).

The portal includes these template pages:

- Home

  The Home page includes the banner image, portal title, Carousel, and navigation to services by
  type and serves as the landing page of the portal. The Carousel offers consumers a preview of the
  most recent services added to the portal. Each card contains the service name, icon, type, and
  description. When selected, developers can view all the information about a service on the Service
  Details page.

- Services

  The Services page contains a catalog of all the services in the portal. Developers can search and
  filter the catalog. When selected, each card in the catalog shows the Service Details page.

- Service Details

  The Service Details page shows the metadata about the service, including the name, description,
  type, version, lifecycle states, and the email of the contact for the service. The details page
  includes the overview and the service documentation. The overview panel reflects the overview page
  created by the admin of the service in Anypoint Exchange. The documentation panel includes an
  interactive console for developers to read the service documentation and test the service.
  Developers can read the summary, methods, endpoints, and authorization sections as defined in the
  API specification.

## Product Integration

API Experience Hub is integrated with the following products:

- Anypoint Exchange

  Exchange is a catalog of reusable assets such as services, policies, agents, APIs, LLMs, and MCP
  servers, API spec fragments, API groups, templates, examples, custom assets, and integration
  assets such as connectors. Within API Experience Hub, portal admins can curate their portals by
  importing services from Exchange. For more information, see the
  [Anypoint Exchange](https://docs.mulesoft.com/exchange/).

- Salesforce Experience Cloud

  API Experience Hub is powered by Salesforce Experience Cloud and provides customization and
  branding capabilities your developer audiences. For more information, see
  [Customizing Your Portal Using Experience Builder](customizing-your-portal).

## Supported Browsers

See the following topics for information about supported browsers:

- [Browser Support for Anypoint Platform](../general/browser-support)
- [Supported Browsers for Experience Cloud Sites](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.networks_communities_browser_support.htm&type=5)

## API Experience Hub on Control Planes

All features of API Experience Hub are supported on Japan and Canada control planes.

## See Also

- [User Roles, Permissions, and Profiles](user-roles-permissions-and-profiles)
- [Getting Started with API Experience Hub](getting-started-with-api-experience-hub)
---

# Licensing

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Licenses are procured and provisioned by MuleSoft. API Experience Hub requires two types of
licenses:

- Salesforce

  This license is required to use the Salesforce Experience Builder.

- External Identity

  This license is based on the number of member requests to access the portal. The current package
  has a minimum of 100 service access requests which require a minimum of 2000 External Identity
  licenses. This is required for the portal consumers to access the portal.

To set up an organization, you can have the following Salesforce editions:

- Unlimited Edition
- Enterprise Edition
- Performance Edition of Salesforce
---

# Limitations

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This release has the following limitations:

- API Experience Hub does not use platform cache.
- API Experience Hub functions at the main organization level only.
- A Salesforce organization canât connect to more than one Anypoint organization.
- Multiple portals per Salesforce organization arenât supported.
- API Groups arenât available.
- The maximum number of user groups is 100.
- New portals can have a maximum of 7,000 services.
- Existing portals can have a maximum of 350 services.

  Donât make changes to the permissions of the out-of-the-box Teams created in Access Management for
  API Experience Hub. Each service added to the portal uses two permissions and Access Management
  has a limit of 1,000 permissions per team.

- Enable scalability on the **Catalog management** page to increase the maximum number of services
  to 7,000 for the portal. After you enable this option, you canât revert to the portal version that
  supports up to 350 services.

  For more information, see
  [Opt in to Increase the Number of Services in the Portal](managing-apis#opt-in-to-increase-the-number-of-services-in-the-portal).

- API Experience Hub isnât certified for use on mobile devices. Some functionality works on mobile
  devices, but some functionality such as the API Console does not.
- IP restrictions arenât supported. For more information, see the [Troubleshooting](troubleshooting)
  section.
- Validation rules canât be applied to standard objects that are created during the Salesforce
  linking process. For more information, see the [Troubleshooting](troubleshooting) section for a
  work around.
- The maximum pending user registration requests is 500. For more information, see
  [Managing Users](managing-users).
- The community portalâs default name API Experience Hub canât be changed. Changing the name of the
  portal generates errors in the configuration.
- You canât change the names of services in API Experience Hub.
- API Experience Hub Lightning custom components support English only. For more information, see
  [API Experience Hub Lightning Components](api-experience-hub-lightning-components)
- Microsoft Azure OpenID Connect isnât supported.
---

# Customizing the Portal Look-and-Feel

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Builder creates a Salesforce Experience Cloud site with your desired brand identity, look and
feel, and site structure by providing control over the siteâs components, themes, and pages, and its
settings for general properties, themes, and SEO.

The Builder link opens your site for editing. You can edit each page in your portal, create new
pages, and add and edit the components on each page.

The Builder interface contains the pages list, component palette, theme menu, page structure menu,
and settings menu.

API Experience Hub isnât certified for use on mobile devices. Some functionality works on mobile
devices, but some functionality such as the API Console does not.

## Pages List

The Pages list shows the pages included with API Experience Hub and the pages you have added and
enables you to select and edit each page. The included page Home is selected by default.

The Pages list enables you to create new pages by using the New Page option. A new page can be an
object page or a standard page. Object pages are used only with Salesforce objects, so a custom
community page starts as a standard page. A standard page can start from one of the preconfigured
pages or as a new blank page.

Make a new blank page by selecting the layout and then entering the new pageâs name and URL. Use
that URL to make links to the new page, including links in menus. The new page displays placeholders
for content based on the layout.

Fill in a new page by adding components from the Components menu, such as a Headline component in
the page header, or an API Experience Hub component such as an API Version Carousel.

The Pages list shows the editable configuration properties for each page, which are described in
[Manage Your Communityâs Pages and Their Properties in Experience Builder](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=experience.community_builder_manage_pages_overview.htm&type=5).

## Components

Clicking the Components lightning bolt icon displays the component palette, which includes
Salesforce Experience Cloud components, such as Headline or Featured Topics, and components specific
to API Experience Hub, such as API Carousel or API Console. You can drag any component from the
component palette to the current page. Select the component on the page to show the componentâs
configuration options.

API Experience Hub has the following Lightning components:

- API Console
- Asset Detail Header
- Asset Documentation Viewer
- Assets Carousel
- Assets Catalog
- Search Box
- Search Result

Detailed component information is available from the page and the Salesforce Experience Cloud
[Components](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=experience.rss_component_reference_table.htm&type=5)
documentation. See
[API Experience Hub Lightning Components](api-experience-hub-lightning-components) for a description
of each component.

## Theme

Clicking the Theme paintbrush icon offers these general branding parameters:

- Colors

  Sets the siteâs general color scheme, including paragraph, heading, and button text.

- Images

  Specifies the siteâs company logo and the login pageâs background Image.

- Fonts

  Specifies the siteâs fonts, which include a primary font and header fonts, and specifies the
  siteâs text case.

- Theme Settings

  You can adjust community styling by editing the CSS rules. Next to the Theme title, click the
  Theme list, and click Edit CSS. Refer to
  [Customize Your Experience Site Theme](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=experience.community_builder_theme_customize.htm&type=5).

  Brand multiple portals using branding sets. For more information, see
  [Use Branding Sets in Experience Builder](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.community_designer_brandsets.htm&type=5).

## Page Structure

Clicking the Page Structure icon, which resembles a bulleted list, shows the components in the
current page and enables you to select and remove them. Selecting a component displays its
configuration menu.

## Settings

Clicking the Settings gear icon offers these parameters:

- [General](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=community_builder_general_settings.htm&type=5)
- [Theme](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=community_builder_theme.htm&type=5)
- SEO

  Search Engine Optimization (SEO) features define what information can be indexed by external
  services and discovered through online searches, and can increase traffic to your public
  community.

Refer to the
[Experience Builder Settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.community_builder_general_settings.htm&type=5)
help topic for more information.

## See Also

[API Experience Hub Lightning Components](api-experience-hub-lightning-components)
---

# Catalog Management

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

From the **Catalog management** page, curate your portal by viewing information about an asset from
Exchange, adding or removing assets, and changing the visibility of a version.

The portal supports these service types:

- Agents
- AsyncAPI
- GraphQL
- gRPC
- HTTP
- LLM
- MCP server
- REST
- SOAP

The **Catalog management** page shows this information:

- **Services in the portal**

  Shows the services that have been added to your portal. Administrators can add only those services
  for which they have Exchange Viewer permission. For more information, see
  [Assigning Permissions](assigning-permissions).

All administrators are automatically assigned the Exchange Viewer permission for all the services
added to the portal. By default, the visibility of the services is set to Administrators. The portal
administrator can change the visibility or remove services in the **Services in the portal** tab.

- **Services in Exchange**

  Shows the services that exist in your Exchange organization but havenât been added to your portal.

## Opt in to Increase the Number of Services in the Portal

Enable portal scalability in the **Catalog management** page. Opt in once for maximum service
support in your portal. After enabling this option, you canât revert to the portal version that
supports up to 350 services. New portals automatically scale to 7,000 services.

1.  Click **Learn more** from the sidebar in **Catalog management**.
2.  Select **I understand and want to enable scalability.** and click **Enable**.

## View Services in Exchange

You can view information about a service directly from Exchange to determine whether you want to add
the service in your portal.

From the **Services in Exchange** tab, select a service from the list and select **View in
Exchange**.

To filter services by categories and tags, select an option from the **Any category** or **Any tag**
dropdown and click **Apply**.

## Add Services from Exchange

Curate your portal in API Experience Hub by adding services from Exchange. You must have Services in
Exchange before you can add them to your portal. When adding services to the portal, the default
visibility is Administrator. You can change the visibility to include members or everyone. See
[Make Versions Visible](#make-versions-visible) for more information.

To add services to your portal:

1.  From the **Catalog management** page, select the **Services in Exchange** tab.
2.  To search for a service by name or description, enter the name or description into the
    **Search** bar.
3.  To filter services by categories and tags, select an option from the **Any category** or **Any
    tag** dropdown and click **Apply**.
4.  Locate the service in the list, click **\+ (Add to portal)**.

    The service displays in the list in the **Services in the portal** tab.

## Remove Services from the Portal

To remove services from your portal:

1.  From the **Catalog management** page, select the **Services in the portal** tab.
2.  To search for a service, enter the name, description, category, or tag keyword into the
    **Search** box.
3.  To filter services by categories and tags, select an option from the **Any category** or **Any
    tag** dropdown and click **Apply**.
4.  Locate the service in the list and click the trash can (Remove from portal).
5.  Click **Confirm**.

    In the **Services in the portal** tab, the service is removed from the list.

## Make Versions Visible

By default, when you add a new service, the minor version shared with an Administrator is the latest
version of the service. The instance visibility is set to Public instances. Only the instances that
are public in Exchange are available for end-users to request access to them.

You can manage which version of a service a user can view in your portal. You can set the visibility
to Administrators, Members and user groups, Guests, or None.

- Administrators

  Use this option if you want the version to be seen by the portal administrator only.

- Members

  Use this option if you want the version to be seen only by logged-in members or specific user
  groups.

- Guests

  Use this option if you want the version to be publicly available to anyone.

- None

  Select no users to hide the version from everyone.

If the visibility is Guests, Members, or user groups, consumer developers can see the new version in
the carousel of the portal.

To edit the user visibility that displays for a service:

1.  From the **Catalog management** page, select **Services in the portal**.
2.  To search for a service, enter the name, description, category, or tag keyword into the
    **Search** box.
3.  To filter services by categories and tags, select an option from the **Any category** or **Any
    tag** dropdown and click **Apply**.
4.  Locate the service in the list and click **Edit visibility**.
5.  Select an instance visibility option:
    - All instances - End-users canât request access.
    - Public instances - End-users can request access.

6.  Expand an API version and select the users who can see the service.
7.  Click **Save**.

## Next Steps

From [User Management](managing-users), you can view and manage users and access requests from guest
users.
---

# Managing Client Applications and Contracts

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Client applications and contracts requested through API Experience Hub are created in API Manager.
Depending on the SLA tier configuration, theyâre approved automatically or they require approval by
the owner of the service or group. To review and approve contracts, refer to the API Manager
documentation.

## See Also

- [My Applications](my-applications)
---

# Managing Users

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

View and manage users, access requests from guest users, create and manage user groups, and manage
how users log in. By default, users can self-register for your portal.

## Manage Portal Members

Manage portal members using the **Portal members** page. When a guest userâs registration or an
existing Salesforce user is approved, the userâs name is added to the list of members. You can view
a searchable list of current members and manage their accounts by enabling or disabling them and
adding them to user groups.

### View and Search for Members

Find members by entering the memberâs name or email address in the **Search by name or email** box.

### Enable or Disable Access to Your Portal

When a userâs access to the portal is approved in the **Pending registration requests** page, the
**Enable portal access** slider is enabled in the **Portal members** page. Disable a userâs access
by moving the slider to off.

### Assign Members to User Groups

Assign users to user groups to manage which services they can see in your portal. You can assign a
user to multiple user groups or change user groups.

1.  From the **Portal members** page, locate the memberâs name in the list and click **View and
    assign user groups**.
2.  Select one or more user groups and click **Apply**.

## Manage Pending Registration Requests

Admins must manually approve or reject requests for users who want to access the portal using the
**Pending registration requests** page. You can view the name, email, registration date, and
registration status of the users who register for access. By default, users are assigned to the
Members profile. You can add them to the available user groups that can see specific services in the
portal. The maximum pending user registration requests is 500.

When the registration is approved, the user is removed from the list on the **Pending registration
requests** page and added to the list of members on the **Portal members** page.

If the registration is rejected, the user is removed from the list and canât log in and access the
portal.

### Approve or Reject Member Registration

After a guest user registers or an existing Salesforce user requests access, approve or reject the
requests and assign the user to user groups. All users who self-register are assigned to the Members
profile as well as existing Salesforce users who request access. You can assign member users to
multiple user groups.

1.  Click **Pending registration requests**.
2.  To find a user, enter the userâs name or email in the **Search by name or email** box.
3.  To assign the user to a user group:
    1.  Click **Members** and select one or more user groups.
    2.  Click **Apply**.

4.  Click **Approve** or **Reject**.

## Manage User Groups

Create and manage a group of users who can see specific services in your portal using the **User
groups** page. The maximum number of user groups is 20.

Before deleting a user group, remove all users, services, and the visibility for all instances
assigned to the user group.

- To create groups of users who can see specific services in the portal:
  1.  From the **User groups** page, click **Create user group**.
  2.  Enter a name for the group and an optional description.
  3.  Click **Save**.

- To delete a user group, from the **User groups** page, select a user group and click **Delete user
  group**.

### Map User Groups to External Identity Providers

To let users of a user group log in to your portal using SSO, map the user group to an existing
external identity provider. To avoid login issues, use the same group name and identity provider
name configured for the identity provider.

To add the user group to the corresponding profile in API Experience Hub:

1.  From the **User groups** page, select a group.
2.  In the **External IdP groups** tab, click **\+ Add group mapping**.
3.  Enter the group name and select a Salesforce identity provider name.
4.  Click **Save**.

### View Services That User Groups Can See

To view the services that are visible by a user group for portal members and also the APIs assigned
only to a user group. The **Services visible to the user group** tab shows all of the APIs that
members of the portal can see.

1.  From the **User groups** page, select a user group.
2.  Select the **Services visible to the user group** tab.
3.  To see services assigned to the group only, turn on **Services assigned to this group**.

To view or change a serviceâs visibility, see
[Make Versions Visible](managing-apis#make-versions-visible).

### Delete Identity Provider Groups

To remove the external identity provider groups that you donât need:

1.  From the **User groups** page, select a user group.
2.  Select the **External IdP groups** tab.
3.  Locate the group in the list and click **Remove group**.

## Manage Login Settings

Disable or enable self-registration for external users and set up single sign-on for your portal
using the **Login settings** page. Self-registration enables guest users to register themselves as
members of your portal. From the **Pending requests** page, you can see a list of pending requests
from guest users who want to access your portal.

### Disable or Enable Self-Registration

By default, the ability for external users to self-register is enabled. This allows guest users to
register, become a member, and access your portal site. Administrators can disable
self-registration.

> [!NOTE] Self-registration is for new users only. Existing users canât self-register. They must use
> an identity provider to access the portal.

To disable external user registration, from the **Login settings** page turn off the **Allow
external user registration** option.

To enable external registration again, turn on the **Allow external user registration** option.

### Configuring Single Sign-On (SSO)

The **Single sign-on (SSO)** section provides information about how to set up your portal with SSO.
For detailed instructions about configuring SSO, see
[Enabling Single Sign-On for Your Portal](enabling-sso-for-your-portal).

### Manage Salesforce Identity Providers

When enabling SSO, you can add new Salesforce identity providers and delete the providers that you
previously configured, and enable and disable them as needed. When the identity provider is enabled,
users can log in to the portal using this identity provider.

To add a new identity provider or delete an existing one:

1.  From the **User management**, select **Login settings**.
2.  From the **Single sign-on (SSO)** section, scroll to the **Add Salesforce identity providers**
    section.
3.  Click **Select identity provider** and select an option from the dropdown menu.
4.  Click **\+ Add identity provider**.

    The identity provider is added to the list.

5.  If you want to delete the identity provider, click **Delete**.

To enable or disable an identity provider:

1.  From the **Single sign-on (SSO)** section, scroll to the **Add Salesforce identity providers**
    section.
2.  Move the slider to **Enabled** next to the name of the identity provider.
3.  If you want to disable the identity provider, move the slider to **Disable**.

## Next Steps

You can further customize and brand your portal using the
[Experience Builder](customizing-your-portal) by clicking **Preview and publish your portal**.

## See Also

- [Registering to Access a Portal](registering-for-access-to-portal)
---

# Managing Your Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After your portal is styled using the guided design, the **Manage your portal** page provides
options to manage services, users, settings, environments, and surfaces issues that occur while
managing the portal.

- [Catalog management](managing-apis)

  Add your services from Exchange into your portal, remove services from your portal, view list of
  services that you have in Exchange, and change the visibility of a version.

- [User Management](managing-users)

  View and manage members and pending requests.

- Settings

  [View information about your portalâs Salesforce account](viewing-salesforce-account-information)
  and [disconnect the portal](disconnecting-the-portal).

- [Environments](working-with-sandboxes)

  Manage separate deployment environments for your portal. You can connect to multiple Salesforce
  sandbox organizations and switch between them.

## Viewing Issues on Catalog Management Pages

API Experience Hub provides diagnostics checks that run in the background to identify issues with
the portal and attempt to fix them automatically or provide troubleshooting options. Checks are
triggered when loading or refreshing a page and performing an action on the UI. These checks help
administrators identify error states and troubleshoot issues.

When issues are detected on Catalog management pages, a message displays in the banner of the page.
The message indicates that there are some detected issues that the system is trying to fix.

![An example of the banner with detected issues](_images/error-message-banner.png)

If there are still issues after all checks and patches are attempted, the banner shows a message
that there are issues that require you to act on. To see more information about these issues, click
**View details**.

The following minimal prerequisite checks run each time pages in the portal are loaded or refreshed:

- Connection with Salesforce is operational.
- The API Experience Hub package is installed in the Salesforce organization.
- Access Management team for the out-of-the-box profiles are created.
- The shared user (Experience Builder preview) exists and has all the necessary permissions to log
  in to Salesforce and interact with the Experience Builder.

Issues can have the following severity:

- Error

  Issues that block you from using one or multiple functions in the portal.

- Warning

  Issues that indicate something isnât working as expected, but you can still use the portal without
  addressing the issues.

- Info

  Information that is helpful for you to know but isnât blocking you from using the portal.
---

# My Applications

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The **My Applications** page enables you to review the lists of applications that hold a contract
with a service. Each client application has a client ID, client secret, description, URL, redirect
URIs, and grant types.

Services that are protected by a client ID enforcement policy require client applications to provide
a client ID and optional client secret. The client ID and client secret credentials are
automatically created when the client application is registered. For more information about
registering an application, see
[Requesting Access to a Service Instance](requesting-access-to-an-api-instance).

## Search for Applications

If the **My Applications** page lists several applications, search for a specific one by using the
Search box. Enter the application name or description of the application and press Enter or Return.
The number of applications that match your search criteria displays in the list of search results.

## View and Copy Client Credentials

To view the details of a client application, from the **My Applications** page, click the
application from the list to display the **Application Details** page.

You can view and copy client credentials as follows:

- Click **View credentials** from the **My Applications** page.
- To copy the client ID or client secret to the clipboard, click the Copy to clipboard icon.
- To show the client secret, click the Show Client Secret icon.
- To hide the client secret, click the Hide Client Secret icon.

## Application Details

To view the details of a client application, from the **My Applications** page, click the
application from the list to display the Application Details page.

You can view the following details about an application:

- Application URL

  Displays the applicationâs internet address. Click the link to go to the URL.

- Client Credentials

  Displays the applicationâs client ID and client secret.

- OAuth 2.0 Redirects URIs

  Lists the applicationâs redirect URIs.

- OAuth 2.0 Grant Type

  Lists the applicationâs grant types.

- Contracts

  From the Contracts section, you can see the applicationâs service name, environment and instances,
  instance URLs, SLA tier, and request access state. The environment can be either a sandbox or
  development environment. Request access states can be `pending`, `approved`, or `failed`.

From the **Application Details** page, you can:

- Reset the client secret.
- Edit the client applications details.
- View the instance URL.
- Add an SLA tier.
- Change the SLA tier.
- Delete the client application.

### Reset Client Secret

You can reset the client secret for an application on a regular cadence to ensure security when
users leave the organization or to ensure that the secret isnât accidentally exposed to unauthorized
users.

From the **Application Details** page, you can reset the client secret for an application.
Applications using the current client secret canât access any of the services with which they were
registered.

To reset the client secret:

1.  From the **Client Credentials** section, click **Reset client secret**.
2.  Click **Confirm**.
3.  To view the new client secret, click the View icon.

### Edit the Client Application Details

You can edit a client application name, description, OAuth 2.0 grant type, OAuth 2.0 Redirect URIs,
and SLA Tier from the Application Details page.

1.  From the **My Applications** section, click the application name in the list.
2.  Click **Edit**.
3.  From the **Application Details** page, edit the corresponding fields.
4.  Click **Save**.

### View and Copy the Instance URL

To view the instance URL for a service by clicking **View URL** and copy it by clicking the **Copy
from clipboard** icon.

### Add an SLA Tier

SLA tiers are defined in API Manager and display as SLA Tier options in the Application Details
page. If an application does not have an SLA tier listed, you can request to add an SLA tier. The
instance owner must approve the tier.

1.  From the **My Applications** section, click the application name in the list.
2.  From the **SLA Tier** column of the **Contracts** section, click + Add.
3.  From the **Add SLA Tier** list, select from the list of tiers.
4.  Click **Save**.

    When the request access is approved, the Request Access State column displays the Approved state
    and an email is sent to the member.

### Change the SLA Tier

The SLA tier can limit the number of requests an application can make to the service. You can
increase or decrease the number of allowed requests. You can change the SLA tier for an application
from the Application Details page. The available SLA tiers are set by an admin in API Manager. The
owner of the API instance must approve the request to change the tier level.

To edit the SLA tier for a client application:

1.  On the **My Applications** page, click the name of the application in the list.
2.  From the **SLA Tier** column of the **Contracts** section that you want to change, click the SLA
    tier edit option.
3.  From the **Edit SLA Tier** dialog, select from the list of SLA tiers.
4.  Click **Save**.
5.  Click **Okay** to close the message.

### Delete a Client Application

When you no longer need the contract for a service, you can delete it. Once the application is
deleted, there is no longer a contract with the service and you canât access it. To regain access,
you must request access to the service again.

To delete a client application:

1.  On the **Application Details** page for the application, click **Delete**.
2.  In the **Delete application** message, click **Delete**.

    The application is removed from the **My Applications** page.
---

# Preview Your Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After making changes to your portal, you can preview the new version to ensure that it meets your
expectations from the **Manage your service** pages or from the Builder:

1.  From the **Manage your portal** pages, click **Preview and publish your portal**.
2.  Verify that your changes are applied and displayed correctly.

    Services that are visible only to administrators appear in this preview.

3.  To publish the portal, click **Publish**.
4.  To make changes, click **Back to Builder**.

## See Also

- [Customizing Your Portal Using the Builder](customizing-your-portal)
---

# Previewing and Publishing Your Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After managing and curating your portal, you can preview your changes in the Builder, then publish
it for your users. The URL for the portal isnât accessible until the portal is published. When you
publish your site for the first time, the portal URL is live and login access is enabled.

To preview and publish the portal, follow these steps:

1.  From the **Manage your portal** page, click **Preview and publish your portal**.
2.  Review your changes and click **Publish**.

    You receive an email confirmation when your changes are live. The email contains the URL link to
    the published site.

## View URL for a Live Published Portal

To view the URL for the live published portal, in the Builder, go to **Settings** > **General**. The
**Published Status** section displays the URL for the live published portal.

## See Also

- [Customizing Your Portal Using the Experience Builder](customizing-your-portal)
- [Discovering and Consuming Services](discovering-and-consuming-apis)
---

# Registering for Access to a Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Users who access a portal without logging in canât perform most actions in the portal, like
requesting access to services to use them. Becoming a member gives you access to more services and
functionality. New users can sign up for an account to become a member. After signing up, API
Experience Hub sends a welcome email that contains a link to set up the account and password.

To register for an account:

1.  In a browser window, enter the URL of the portal.
2.  Click **Log in**.
3.  Click **Not a Member?**.
4.  Enter your first name, last name, and email address.
5.  Click **Sign Up**.
6.  Follow the instructions in the welcome email to set up your account.

## See Also

- [Discovering Services](discovering-apis)
- [Searching for Services](searching-for-apis)
---

# Requesting Access to a Portal for Salesforce Users

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Salesforce users can request portal access when they donât have Single Sign-On (SSO) configured.

Before you request access, confirm that you donât have a Single Sign-On (SSO) configuration. Having
an SSO configuration can cause issues in the portal. For example, you might not see all services
available to you if they are not available in the SSO configuration.

If the request access component is available on a portal, Salesforce users can request access. Users
can request access by entering their username and clicking the **Requests access** button or the
**Submit request** button.

When the request is approved, an email is sent with login information.

## See Also

- [Discovering Services](discovering-apis)
- [Searching for Services](searching-for-apis)
---

# Requesting Access to a Service Instance

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

From the Service Details page, member users request access to an instance to which to register a
client application. They do this by selecting an instance, selecting or creating a client
application, and optionally selecting a service-level agreement (SLA). When the owner of the
instance approves the request, a contract is created between the client application and the
instance, and the client application is registered.

Instances that the client ID enforcement policy protects require client applications to provide a
client ID and an optional client secret. Once the request for access is approved, the client
application is registered and the client ID and client secret credentials are automatically created.
Member users can request access to an instance of a service that is published in the portal. These
users can either create a new application or use an application that they already created to request
access.

If terms and conditions are set for an asset, youâre prompted to accept the terms before access is
granted. The created contract includes the terms and conditions.

## Before You Begin

Before you can request access to a service, you must be a member user and be logged into the portal.
See [Registering for Access to a Portal](registering-for-access-to-portal)

## Use an Existing Application to Request Access to a Service

You can request access to a service by using an existing application:

1.  Click a service to open the **Service Details** page.
2.  Click **Request access**.
3.  In the instance list, select an instance.
4.  In the **Client application** list, select an application.

    An application and its instance use the same client provider. If an organization administrator
    has configured multiple client providers in Anypoint API Manager, then the application list
    shows only the applications that use the same client provider as the selected instance.

5.  From **SLA tier**, select a service-level agreement (SLA) value for the application.

    The SLA tier appears only when the instance has one or more tiers.

6.  If the service has terms and conditions, select **I accept the terms and conditions**.
7.  Click **Request access**.

    A Request access message displays to inform you that the request was received and is pending. If
    the SLA tier has auto-approval, the message shows that the request access was approved, and the
    application displays in the My Applications page.

8.  Click **Okay** to close the message.

    If the SLA tier has auto-approval, the application is received and approved.

9.  Click **Okay** to close the message.
10. To see the application details, go to the **My Applications** page.

    The **My Applications** page lists the client ID and client secret for this access instance.

## Create a New Application to Request Access to a Service

If you do not have an application to use to request access, create a new application:

1.  From the Catalog, click a service to open the **Service Details** page.
2.  Click **Request access**.
3.  From the **Service instance** list, select an instance.
4.  From the **Client application** options, select **Create a new application**.
5.  From the **Name** field, enter a client application name.
6.  From the **Description** field, enter a description of the client application.
7.  From the **OAuth 2.0 Grant Type** options, if there are grant types for the application, select
    a grant type.
8.  From the **OAuth 2.0 Redirect URIs** field, enter a URI address from.
9.  From the **Application URL** field, enter a URL.
10. From the **SLA tier** options, select a service-level agreement (SLA) value for the application.
11. Click **Request access**.

    If auto-approval is enabled for the SLA tier, a message displays that the application is
    approved. If auto-approval isnât enabled, a message displays that the request is pending.

12. Click **Okay** to close the message.

    When the application is approved, an email is sent.

13. Click **Okay** to close the message.
14. To see the application details, go to the **My Applications** page from the portal.

    The **My Applications** page lists the client ID and client secret for this access instance.

## See Also

- [Viewing Service Details](viewing-api-details)
- [My Applications](my-applications)
---

# Searching for Services

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

From the **Home** and **Services** pages, search the catalog by a service name to find a specific
service. Enter at least four characters in the **Search all services** box then select an option
from the results.

![Search box to enter a service name](_images/aeh-search-by-api-name-box.png)

Filter the service by category and type to further refine your search results. The filter options
display depending on which service categories and types are available in the portal.

To filter by categories or tags, select one or more options from **All categories** or **All service
types** and click **Apply**.

From the **Home** page, you can also filter the carousel by clicking on any of the service type
cards.

![Service cards you can select to filter results by service type](_images/aeh-api-type-filter.png)

## See Also

- [Viewing Service Details](viewing-api-details)
---

# Setting Up the Portal

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After connecting to your Salesforce organization, the guided setup takes portal administrators
through the process of setting up the portal, including creating and styling it using out-of-the-box
templates.

You can create a new portal, use an existing portal to create the new portal, or create the portal
later. The option to create a new portal uses an existing portal that was disconnected from a
Salesforce organization. The new portal uses the styling metadata from the existing portal.
Selecting this option from the guided setup enables you to quickly set up the portal without going
through the styling process. Because you can have only one Salesforce organization per portal, the
existing portal is overwritten with the new portal for that Salesforce organization.

The guided design contains basic styling options for the Home, Catalog, and Service Details pages
with a live preview to help you visualize your portal. Each page includes a progress bar to see
where you are in the portal design process.

Styling a portal includes the following tasks:

1.  Style the Home page

    The Home page is the landing page of your portal and is also known as the Carousel. The template
    includes adding a logo, banner image, org branding, banner title, description of the portal, and
    footer configuration.

2.  Style the Catalog

    The Services page contains the collection of services in your portal and is known as the
    Catalog. The template includes customizing the colors for each service type like agents, APIs,
    LLMs, and MCP servers.

3.  Style the Service Details page

    The Service Details page displays information about a service. The template includes the name,
    type of service, owner email address, and a description of the service.

## Set Up a Portal

Set up a portal by choosing how to create the portal.

1.  From the **Set up portal** page of the guided setup, select an option to create the portal:
    1.  To create a new portal, click **Create new portal**.
    2.  To create a new portal using an existing portalâs styling selections, click **Use existing
        portal** and click **Override and create new portal**.
    3.  To create the portal at a later time, click **Set up portal later**.

## Style Your Portal

Follow the guided design and click **Next** to advance through the steps.

> [!NOTE] If you refresh the page or leave the guided design, the progress in the guided setup does
> not persist. You must complete all of the steps again.

## Before You Begin

Before styling your portal, you need the following:

- Image of your company logo
- Color scheme for the portal
- Fonts for company branding
- Image to use as a header for your portal

## Style Portal Homepage

Follow the guided steps to style the homepage:

1.  To add a company logo, drag or upload an image file to **Upload company logo**.

    The supported file formats are PNG, JPEG, SVG, and GIF.

2.  To add a banner image, drag or upload an image file to **Select banner image**.

    The supported file formats are PNG, JPEG, SVG, and GIF.

3.  To brand your org with fonts and colors, from **Customize org branding**:
    - Select a font style from the drop-down menu.
    - Select a font color for the banner image from the color picker.
    - Select a page background color from the color picker.

4.  To add a title for your portal banner, enter the title in the **Enter portal banner title** text
    box.
5.  To add a description for your portal, enter the description in the **Enter portal description**
    text box.
6.  To add footer text and a URL, enter the footer text and URL in the corresponding text boxes from
    **Configure footer**.
7.  Click **Next** to style the Catalog.

## Style Catalog

Follow the guided steps to style the Catalog:

1.  To customize the color of specific services, select a color from the color picker for the
    corresponding service type from **Customize colors**.
2.  To add a banner title for your Catalog, enter the title in the **Enter catalog banner title**
    text box.
3.  To add a description for your Catalog, enter the description in the **Enter catalog
    description** text box.
4.  Click **Next** to style the Service Details page.

## Style Service Details

Follow the guided steps to style the Service Details page:

1.  To show the service ownerâs email, enable **Service Contact** from **Customize service
    information**.
2.  To show client applications on the **Service Details** page, enable **Service Clients** from
    **Customize service information**.
3.  To add a banner title to the Service Details page, enter the title in the **Enter service detail
    banner Title** text box.
4.  To add a description of the Service Details page, enter the description in the **Enter service
    detail description** text box.
5.  If youâre not done styling your portal, click **Previous** to change the styling on previous
    pages.
6.  If youâre done styling your portal, click **Save changes**.

    The process of saving your styles and creating the portal can take several minutes. You can
    navigate away from the page.

## Default Settings

The following settings are set by default when the portal is created:

- Self-registration for guest users is enabled by default
- Status of the portal is active.

## Next Steps

Now that your portal is created, you can manage services by adding them to the portal, manage users,
view settings information, and disconnect a portal from the
[Manage your portal](managing-your-portal) page.

## See Also

- [Managing Your Portal](managing-your-portal)
- [Troubleshooting](troubleshooting)
---

# Simulating API Calls Using the Mocking Service

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To simulate calls to your API, use the mocking service in the API Console of the portal, you can
simulate calls to your API. The mocking service is a feature of Anypoint that enables you to
generate simulated servers to help you develop and test APIs. These mock servers imitate the actions
of accepting requests and providing responses. The mocking service replicates the functionality of a
genuine API.

## Before You Begin

You must have an API specification with at least one endpoint and one method in it.

## Simulate a Call to an API Using an Internal URL

To access the internal URL, you must specify the authorization headers as part of the request URL.
To test an API without supplying authorization headers, generate a mocking service public URL for
this project. The mocking service is the selected server in the **Try it** pane.

1.  On the **Home** or the **Services** page, select a REST API.
2.  Select the **API specification** tab.
3.  Click a method like `GET`.
4.  In the **Documentation** pane, review the requests and responses data.
5.  In the **Try it** pane, enter query parameters you want to test.

    You can enter query parameters as name-value pairs.

6.  Click **Send**.

    API Console displays the HTTP return code as specified in your API. Alternately, you can test
    the API by copying the Request URL and paste it into a web browser or Postman.

## See Also

- [Using the API Console](using-the-api-console)
